rationale: Encodes TDP-43; cytoplasmic aggregation is defining sALS pathology
Most advanced ALS clinical target (QRL-201 Phase 1/2); TDP-43 cryptic exon truncation
Strongest ALS GWAS hit; TDP-43 directly represses UNC13A cryptic exon — direct genotype-to-pathology link
Co-localises with TDP-43 aggregates specifically in Betz cells; prior analysis shows dose-response
Dynactin anchor for retrograde transport; GWAS-supported; mutations cause ALS-FTD
Cytoplasmic dynein heavy chain; motor partner for DCTN1; top TxD gene
Constitutively active variants >5× enriched in ALS patients; executes NAD+-depletion-dependent axon degeneration
GWAS + rare variant burden; disrupts DNA repair, microtubule homeostasis, and nucleocytoplasmic transport in ALS iPSC motor neurons
GWAS + rare burden; loss-of-function in ALS; phosphorylates OPTN to activate autophagy receptor function
Autophagy receptor directly downstream of TBK1; GWAS hit; co-localises with TDP-43 inclusions
Housekeeping gene — negative control; should produce minimal directional shift
